title: Myanmar Civil War Update — Apr 27, 2026
summary: On Day 1,911, the resistance launched the Steering Council for the Emergence of a Federal Democratic Union (SCEF) — the most unified anti-junta political-military council since the 2021 coup, uniting the NUG, CRPH, KIO, KNU, KNPP, and CNF. This followed China FM Wang Yi's April 26 conclusion of his Naypyidaw visit, pledging to 'firmly support Myanmar's national sovereignty' and expanding China-junta security cooperation. Falam, Chin State's capital, was recaptured by the SAC on April 25-26 after a year of resistance control, costing the junta 500+ casualties while resistance forces withdrew with 300+ weapons. SAC drone strikes killed 8 civilians including 3 children on April 25-26 in Karenni State and Magway Region; on April 24, SAC jets and Mi-35 helicopters killed 12+ civilians in Bon Taung village, Karen State. KNLA launched an assault on Waw Lay military base near Myawaddy on April 27, forcing junta troops to flee. Map lines and points updated for all new strikes and resistance operations; KPIs updated to Day 1,911.

title: Myanmar Civil War Tracker Update — Sun Apr 26, 2026
summary: SAC Recaptures Falam Town and Surbung Airport After Year-Long Resistance Hold — 500+ Military Casualties, Resistance Withdraws With 300+ Weapons. Wang Yi Concludes Myanmar Visit — China Pledges to 'Firmly Support' Junta Sovereignty; Trade and Security Cooperation Expanded. SAC Drones Kill 8 Civilians Including 3 Children in Karenni State and Magway — Novice Monk Among Dead.

title: Myanmar Civil War Update — Apr 25, 2026
summary: On Day 1,909 of Myanmar's civil war, the junta imposed martial law on 60 towns across six states, authorizing military courts with death penalty powers — one of the most sweeping legal crackdowns since the coup. China FM Wang Yi arrived in Naypyidaw for a two-day visit with Min Aung Hlaing, delivering Beijing's critical endorsement of the SAC's 'civilian rule' transition. On April 23, SAC forces mortared an IDP camp in Hpakant Township, killing 13-year-old Maung Swut Jet Aung while studying — collective punishment during the KIA's sustained Hpakant offensive. A junta ceremony claiming 517 resistance surrenders on April 24 was dismissed as fabricated propaganda by Naypyidaw PDF commanders. Map lines updated for Hpakant IDP camp mortar strike; KPIs updated to Day 1,909.

title: Myanmar Civil War Update — Apr 24, 2026
summary: On Day 1,908 of Myanmar's civil war, The Irrawaddy documented that the SAC has killed 100+ civilians through airstrikes since the junta parliament convened March 16 — exposing 'civilian rule' as a facade for intensified aerial violence. April 23 saw SAC deploy ~10 fighter jets in a major assault on Falam Township, Chin State, while a separate SAC suicide drone bombed a village school in Wetlet (11 injured including 10 students), and jets bombed Ngapali tourist town in Rakhine. On April 22, resistance forces captured a junta outpost in Lewe Township, Naypyidaw Union Territory (10 soldiers including a major killed), SAC jets killed 4 young civilians at a fishing area in Letpadan (Bago), and approximately 50 bombs were dropped on Paletwa Township (Chin State). MoeMaKa confirmed the SAC has now exceeded 9,400 total airstrike incidents since the 2021 coup. Map points and lines updated for Wetlet, Ngapali, Falam, Letpadan, Lewe, and Paletwa.

title: Myanmar Civil War Update — Apr 23, 2026
summary: On Day 1,907 of Myanmar's civil war, the KIA announced the indefinite closure of the Hpakant–Nantyar–Karmaing road — a strategic ground corridor in Kachin State — tightening its stranglehold on the junta's jade-mining garrison and cutting SAC resupply routes. Min Aung Hlaing told Thailand's Foreign Minister he is 'considering good things' for Aung San Suu Kyi (imprisoned 1,907 days), a comment met with deep skepticism by human rights groups given the junta's record. Added to April 22 events: the Kawthoolei Army's (KTLA) pre-dawn ambush of KNLA Battalion 12 soldiers in Karen State, killing 4 soldiers and a 10-year-old child — highlighting the SAC's strategy of weaponizing Karen splinter groups. April 21 events also updated with SAC jets conducting 8 strikes on Taze Township, Sagaing. Map points and lines updated for Taze, Kasay Doh, and the Hpakant road closure corridor.

title: Myanmar Civil War Update — Apr 22, 2026
summary: International pressure on Myanmar's junta escalated on April 22 as diplomatic fallout from the April 20 cross-border hospital bombing in Bue So (Karen State) continued — a SAC bomb landed in Thailand's Mae Hong Son Province during an attack that killed six Arakan Army members, prompting Bangkok to demand explanation. The KNU and CNF publicly rejected Min Aung Hlaing's July peace talks invitation as illegitimate. Internationally, Aung San Suu Kyi's continued imprisonment drew scrutiny: while former President U Win Myint was freed in the Thingyan amnesty, Suu Kyi's sentence was only reduced (not commuted), and 22,668 political prisoners remain detained. Day 1,906 of the Myanmar conflict.

title: Myanmar Civil War Update — Apr 21, 2026
summary: On April 21, a massive fire and explosion at the Homalin river port in Sagaing region killed 2 people and injured 11, destroying 22 motorized boats — the second deadly port fire at Homalin in 2026 and a marker of civilian infrastructure collapse in conflict-ravaged Sagaing. The previous day's cross-border bombing of a KNU hospital (one bomb landing in Thailand's Mae Hong Son) continues to generate diplomatic pressure on the junta. KPIs updated with day 1,905 since the February 2021 coup. The map now includes Homalin as a humanitarian incident point in Sagaing.

title: Myanmar Civil War Update — Apr 20, 2026
summary: On April 20, Myanmar Air Force Yak-130 and MiG-29 jets bombed a KNU hospital in Bue So (Karen State) near the Thai border — 9 bombs were dropped, one crossing into Thailand's Mae Hong Son; 6 Arakan Army members were killed in the strike. On April 19, KIA killed ~40 pro-junta militia in a Hpakant landmine ambush; the SAC bombed Mrauk-U and Ponnagyun injuring 13 civilians; and the IPU reaffirmed CRPH recognition as Myanmar's principal parliamentary body. On April 18, KIA captured 2 militia bases in Tanai Township (Kachin) and a landmine attack killed 5 SAC soldiers in Natmauk (Magway). The Thingyan period saw 110+ aerial attacks on Ayadaw Township (Sagaing) and a 17-year-old detainee tortured and killed in Yesagyo. Map lines, map points, KPIs, and political section updated.

title: Myanmar Civil War Tracker Update — Sun Apr 19, 2026
summary: KIA Landmine Ambush Kills ~40 Pro-Junta Militia in Hpakant — Major Single-Action Losses for SAC. On April 19, 2026, Myanmar Air Force aircraft dropped 500-pound bombs on Mrauk-U and Ponnagyun townships in Rakhine State, injuring at least 13 civilians including 3 monks, 8 men, and 2 women. Inter-Parliamentary Union Confirms CRPH as Myanmar's Principal Parliamentary Representative.

title: Myanmar Civil War Update — Apr 18, 2026
summary: TNLA/PSLF issued a statement on April 15 welcoming Min Aung Hlaing's government, fracturing the Three Brotherhood Alliance under apparent Chinese diplomatic pressure — even as the NUG, KIO, KNU, KNPP, and CNF deepened resistance coordination through the new Steering Council for Federal Democratic Union (SCEF). During Myanmar's Thingyan Water Festival (April 13–17), the SAC bombed Seik Hkun Monastery (killing 2 monks), deployed gyrocopters that killed an infant and a 14-year-old girl in Sagaing, burned ~200 homes in Pakokku Township (Magway), and struck Pauk Township on New Year's Day killing 6. Intense fighting and airstrikes continued in Hpakant (Kachin) for a third consecutive day on April 13. The junta's Thingyan amnesty released RFA contributor Shin Daewe after 915 days in detention.

title: Myanmar Civil War Tracker Update — Fri Apr 17, 2026
summary: SAC Airstrikes Kill Six During Thingyan New Year — Pauk Township (Magway) and Sagaing Hit. Junta Thingyan Amnesty Releases RFA Contributor Shin Daewe After 915 Days — 1,350+ Prisoners Freed.

title: Myanmar Civil War Tracker Update — Thu Apr 16, 2026
summary: PDF Ambush Kills 2 Junta Troops in Setottara Township, Sagaing — Final Day of Thingyan. Ayadaw Township in central Sagaing Region was subjected to over 110 aerial attacks by Myanmar military aircraft across the Thingyan Water Festival period (April 13–17, 2026), including on April 16. A 17-year-old detained by Myanmar junta forces in Yesagyo Township, Magway Region, was tortured and killed between April 14–16, 2026; the victim's body was subsequently dumped in a river.

title: Myanmar Civil War Tracker Update — Wed Apr 15, 2026
summary: TNLA/PSLF Issues Statement Welcoming Min Aung Hlaing's Government — Major Three Brotherhood Alliance Fracture. Steering Council for Federal Democratic Union (SCEF) — NUG, KIO, KNU, KNPP, CNF Form Unified Resistance Command.

title: Myanmar Civil War Tracker Update — Tue Apr 14, 2026
summary: Four Unidentified Bodies Found at Hpakant Junction — Gunshot and Blunt Force Wounds.

title: Myanmar Civil War Tracker Update — Mon Apr 13, 2026
summary: On April 13 — the eve of Myanmar's Thingyan Water Festival — SAC jet aircraft bombed Seik Hkun village west of Shwebo Township in Sagaing Region, striking a monastery and nearby school. Fighting continued in Hpakant Township, Kachin State on April 13, extending from the escalation that began April 11. On April 13, Myanmar military forces burned approximately 200 homes to the ground in North Lukan village in Pakokku Township, Magway Region — located approximately 5 miles from Pakokku airfield. On April 13, the Myanmar military's Northwestern Regional Military Command deployed gyrocopter aircraft to attack villages in Sagaing Region during the Thingyan Water Festival.

title: Myanmar Civil War Update — Apr 12, 2026
summary: KIA and KPDF forces struck a junta strategic hilltop in Hpakant with drone bombs on April 11; the SAC retaliated within 30 minutes by bombing civilian areas including Hmawsi Khtaung ward and Cemetery Hill. Three civilians were found tortured and executed in Chaungkan Village, Pale Township (Sagaing) after being abducted as human shields. Joint resistance forces killed 8 SAC troops in the Ngape Township Kinpwonchaung Gate raid on April 10; the Launglone Steel Butterfly Force killed 2 more in a Launglon police station drone strike the same night. The AA commander declared decisive battles must be won before 2027 as the Sittwe siege tightens to within 2 km of the city center. NUG spokesperson Nay Phone Latt rejected Min Aung Hlaing's presidency: 'The revolution will continue with great momentum.'

title: Myanmar Civil War Tracker Update — Sat Apr 11, 2026
summary: KIA/KPDF Drone Strike on Junta Hilltop — SAC Retaliates with Airstrikes on Hpakant Civilian Areas. Bilin Township (Mon State) Under Sustained Airstrikes — ~40 Bombs Dropped on Villages Since April 7. On April 11, NUG spokesperson Nay Phone Latt issued a clear rejection of Min Aung Hlaing's April 10 presidential inauguration: 'The Myanmar people do not accept it.

title: Breaking: Min Aung Hlaing Sworn In as President — China and India Attend
summary: Myanmar junta chief Min Aung Hlaing officially sworn in as president on April 10, formalizing military rule as a nominal civilian government. China and India attended the inauguration ceremony, conferring regional legitimacy on a transition condemned by the NUG, UN, US, and EU.

title: Myanmar Civil War Update — Apr 09, 2026
summary: China and India confirmed attendance at Min Aung Hlaing's presidential inauguration (April 10), conferring regional legitimacy on the junta's cosmetic civilian transition condemned by the NUG and most democracies. On April 7, SAC jets bombed an IDP displacement camp in Shwebo Township (Sagaing), killing a monk teaching displaced children; sustained fighting in Minbu-Saku entered Day 6 with a school bombed; resistance ambushes in Ye and Mudon townships (Mon State) killed four SAC troops. The KIA's Hpakant offensive continued April 7-8, with SAC shelling injuring six civilians including children and multiple roads closed. SAC airstrikes against Wuntho Township villages continued on April 9 as the junta prepared its presidential ceremony.

title: Myanmar Civil War Tracker Update — Wed Apr 8, 2026
summary: Intense fighting continued in Hpakant Township, Kachin State into April 8 following the KIA's April 2 capture of six SAC frontline positions near Maw Wan Gyi.

title: Myanmar Civil War Tracker Update — Tue Apr 7, 2026
summary: SAC Jets Bomb IDP Displacement Camp in Shwebo Township — Monk Killed, Two Women Injured. Sustained Combat Enters Day 6 in Minbu-Saku Area, Magway — School Bombed, 10+ SAC Troops Injured. Resistance forces conducted two separate combat operations in Mon State on April 7, killing a total of four SAC soldiers.

title: Myanmar Civil War Update — Apr 06, 2026
summary: On April 3, Myanmar's military-dominated parliament elected junta chief Min Aung Hlaing as the country's 11th president with 429 of 584 votes, completing the junta's cosmetic 'civilianization' transition; inauguration is expected this week. The Irrawaddy documented that at least 100 civilians were killed in SAC airstrikes in the period since parliament convened on March 16. Concurrent resistance operations intensified across multiple fronts: the KIA captured six SAC frontline positions in Hpakant Township on April 2; the KNLA killed five SAC soldiers at a railway gate outpost; SAC jets struck PDF units surrounding oil fields in Myaing-Pauk (Magway), killing five fighters; and 17 SAC troops were killed across Kani (Sagaing) and Natmauk (Magway) on April 5. The Arakan Army continues its Sittwe siege with fighters positioned 2 km from the city center.

title: Myanmar Civil War Tracker Update — Sun Apr 5, 2026
summary: Resistance Clashes Kill 17 SAC Troops in Kani (Sagaing) and Natmauk (Magway).

title: Myanmar Civil War Tracker Update — Sat Apr 4, 2026
summary: SAC Jets Strike PDF Units in Myaing–Pauk Oil Field Area — Five Resistance Fighters Killed. Clashes between junta troops and resistance forces broke out around 5:00 PM on April 4 in Mudon Township, Mon State.

title: Myanmar Civil War Tracker Update — Fri Apr 3, 2026
summary: On April 3, Myanmar's military-dominated parliament elected junta chief Min Aung Hlaing as Myanmar's 11th President, receiving 429 of 584 votes cast. Revolutionary forces ambushed a Myanmar military council column in Chauk Township, Magway Region, killing five soldiers.

title: Myanmar Civil War Tracker Update — Thu Apr 2, 2026
summary: The Kachin Independence Army (KIA) captured six Myanmar military council frontline positions near Maw Wan Gyi in Hpakant Township, Kachin State, following intense clashes that began on April 1. Karen National Liberation Army (KNLA) forces attacked a Myanmar military council railway gate outpost in the Ledo area, killing five SAC soldiers.

title: Myanmar Civil War Update — Apr 01, 2026
summary: Min Aung Hlaing was elected vice-president on April 1 by Myanmar's junta-packed lower house with 247 of 260 votes, completing the formal step before a predetermined presidential elevation. He had retired as military Commander-in-Chief on March 30 — installing loyalist General Ye Win Oo — to meet the civilian candidacy requirement under the 2008 constitution. The Arakan Army maintained its Sittwe siege through March 31 with approximately 48 junta soldiers deserting as fighters held positions 2 km from the city center. On March 30, SAC jets killed approximately 10 civilians including pregnant women in Butalin Township, Sagaing Region in a 75-minute airstrike — continuing the documented pattern of deliberate civilian targeting. The days-since-coup counter reached 1,885.

title: Myanmar Civil War Tracker Update — Tue Mar 31, 2026
summary: On March 31, Myanmar's junta-controlled lower house formally nominated Min Aung Hlaing as a vice-presidential candidate, alongside Kyaw Swe (National Unity Party) as a second lower-house nominee. Arakan Army Maintains Sittwe Siege — 48 Junta Troops Desert as AA Tightens Cordon.

title: Myanmar Civil War Tracker Update — Mon Mar 30, 2026
summary: Min Aung Hlaing Retires as Military C-in-C — Parliament Launches Presidential Process. On March 30, three SAC fighter jets conducted a sustained airstrike on Ngapayin village in Butalin Township, Sagaing Region, between approximately 1:00 PM and 2:15 PM local time.

title: Myanmar Civil War Update — Mar 29, 2026
summary: The Arakan Army launched a major assault on the Shwe Min Gan Naval Base outside Sittwe on March 23, killing an estimated 30–40 junta soldiers and pushing fighters to within 2 km of the Rakhine State capital — one of the last significant SAC holdouts in the state. Concurrent AA attacks on Ann Township and Ngape Township on March 24 and an expanding Magway Region offensive (Padan Township, March 29) reflect a broadening multi-front strategy. On the political front, the junta's military-packed parliament convened March 16 and Vice-Senior General Soe Win announced on Armed Forces Day (March 27) that Min Aung Hlaing would be elevated to president within days in a widely condemned cosmetic 'civilian' rebrand. AAPP updated the junta death toll to 7,941+ killed as of March 23, while ACLED's cumulative airstrike count reached 5,912 since the 2021 coup; resistance forces struck back on March 25 with 11 rockets targeting Magway Air Base. A M4.2 aftershock struck near Mandalay on the first anniversary of the Great Sagaing Earthquake (March 28, 2025) as 16.2 million people remain in humanitarian need.

title: Myanmar Civil War Tracker Update — Sat Mar 28, 2026
summary: One Year Since Great Sagaing Earthquake — M4.2 Aftershock Strikes Near Mandalay.

title: Myanmar Civil War Tracker Update — Fri Mar 27, 2026
summary: SAC Armed Forces Day: Soe Win Announces Imminent Presidential Selection. Reporting from March 27 confirmed that Arakan Army forces continued pressing their assault on Sittwe and surrounding areas, with fighters now positioned within approximately 2 km of the city center.

title: Myanmar Civil War Update — Mar 26, 2026
summary: The Myanmar junta convened its first parliament since the 2021 coup on March 16, seating a military-dominated legislature following widely condemned elections; the NUG, ASEAN, and international experts denounced the proceedings as a staged 'civilian' rebrand. On March 8, junta jets struck an Arakan Army POW detention camp in Ann Township, killing 116 people in the deadliest such strike on record. A March 24 Al Jazeera investigation documented how Russian-supplied drones and 'meat assault' tactics drawn from Ukraine are now materially shaping the junta's military campaign, with ACLED recording 5,912 airstrikes since 2021 and air-attack deaths rising 52% in 2025. Sustained aerial strikes continued into the final week of March, hitting villages across Nyaunglebin, Yaw region, and Magway, injuring at least six civilians on March 25 alone. A March 2 mass amnesty of over 10,000 prisoners — the largest in the conflict's history — was widely interpreted as a political maneuver to court ASEAN recognition, with Aung San Suu Kyi notably excluded.

title: Myanmar Civil War Tracker Update — Wed Mar 25, 2026
summary: On March 25, 2026, the SAC military continued its aerial bombardment campaign with airstrikes recorded in Nyaunglebin (Bago Region) and the Yaw region. At 11:20 PM on March 25, the Brave Warriors for Myanmar (BWM) and No More Dictatorship PDF (NMD-PDF) fired 11 rounds of 107mm rockets at Magway Air Base (BWM Mission 53). NUG Representative Meets French Senate — Seeks Concrete Support.

title: Myanmar Civil War Tracker Update — Tue Mar 24, 2026
summary: On the afternoon of March 24, the SAC launched an unprovoked airstrike against the Maulay junction area on the border of Gangaw and Htilin townships in Yaw region (Magway Region). Analysis: Russian Weapons and 'Meat Assault' Tactics From Ukraine Now Shaping Myanmar War. AA Launches Concurrent Assaults on Ann and Ngape — Coordinated Multi-Front Rakhine Offensive. PDF Landmine Ambush Kills 2 Junta Soldiers in Minhla Township.

title: Myanmar Civil War Tracker Update — Mon Mar 23, 2026
summary: On March 23, a Myanmar Air Force jet dropped four bombs on Myaung Township in Sagaing Region, killing at least 4 civilians and burning more than 40 homes. On March 23, two junta suicide drones launched by Light Infantry Battalion 439 struck Noboekhi village in Mone Township, Bago Region (near KNU Kler Lwee Htoo District). Arakan Army Launches Major Assault on Sittwe Naval Base — 30–40 Junta Troops Killed.

title: Myanmar Civil War Tracker Update — Sun Mar 22, 2026
summary: On March 22, two Myanmar Air Force jets conducted multiple airstrikes on Ayardaw Township in Sagaing Region, killing at least 6 civilians.

title: Myanmar Civil War Tracker Update — Sat Mar 21, 2026
summary: Junta Airstrikes Hit Five Villages in Nyaunglebin District, Bago Region. International Experts Call for Global Rejection of Junta's 'Puppet Parliament'.

title: Myanmar Civil War Tracker Update — Mon Mar 16, 2026
summary: Junta-Backed Parliament Convenes for First Time Since 2021 Coup.

title: Myanmar Civil War Tracker Update — Sun Mar 8, 2026
summary: Junta Bombs Arakan Army POW Camp — 116 Killed in Ann Township.

title: Myanmar Civil War Tracker Update — Mon Mar 2, 2026
summary: Senior General Min Aung Hlaing authorized the release of 10,162 inmates — 7,337 convicted under Myanmar's counterterrorism law — to mark Peasants' Day, two weeks before parliament was set to convene.
